editorial.title: 핵잠수함 10년 계획 첫발... 넘어야 할 산 많다
editorial.body: (사설) 정부가 2030년대 중반 국내 기술로 핵추진 잠수함 개발을 추진한다. 이는 안보 강화와 4만 개 일자리 창출, 방위산업 역량 강화 등 경제·안보 파급 효과가 기대된다. 핵 비확산 의무 준수와 국제 협력, 기술 확보도 강조된다. |

editorial.titleTr: Nuclear Submarine Plan Takes First Step... Steep Hurdles Ahead
summaryTr: 정부, 2030년대 중반 국내 기술로 핵추진 잠수함 개발 추진
4만 개 일자리 창출과 방위산업 역량 강화 기대
핵 비확산 의무 준수하며 국제 협력 강조
editorial.bodyTr: The government is pushing to develop a domestically-engineered nuclear-powered submarine by the mid-2030s. This initiative is expected to yield significant ripple effects in both the economy and security, including strengthened national defense, the creation of approximately 40,000 jobs, and enhanced defense industry capabilities. The plan also emphasizes compliance with nuclear non-proliferation obligations, international cooperation, and securing the necessary technology.

editorial.body: 분배 주장 노동장관, 세계 반도체 경쟁 생각이나 해봤나 김영훈 노동부 장관이 대기업 초과 이익을 사회적으로 재분배할 방법을 찾기 위해 긴급 토론회를 열 것이라고 밝혔다. 김 장관은 전통적 문법을 뛰어넘어 발생한 초과 이익에 대해 세금, 판매·관리비, 재무적 비용

editorial.titleTr: Labor Minister's "Distribution" Push: Does He Even Think About Global Chip Competition?
summaryTr: Labor Minister Advocates for Redistribution: Has He Considered Global Semiconductor Competition? Labor Minister Kim Young-hoon announced he will hold an emergency forum to find ways to socially redistribute excess profits of large corporations.
Minister Kim stated that taxes, sales and administrative expenses, and financial costs should be applied to excess profits that have accrued beyond traditional economic frameworks.
editorial.bodyTr: The Labor Minister advocating redistribution—has he given any thought to global semiconductor competition? Labor Minister Kim Young-hoon announced that he will hold an emergency forum to find ways to socially redistribute excess profits of large corporations. Minister Kim stated that regarding excess profits generated beyond traditional economic frameworks, taxation, sales and administrative expenses, and financial costs should be considered.

editorials.body: 부동산 쉽다던 게 얼마 전인데, 장관들에 집값 대책 촉구 이재명 대통령이 26일 국무회의에서 장관과 참모들에게 집값이 다시 오른다는데 대책을 세우고 있느냐고 말했다. 이 대통령은 정부 정책에 대한 국민 신뢰가 중요하다고도 했다. 회의에서는 삼성전자의 고액 성과급

부동산이 쉽다던 발언과 달리 현실은 가격 상승으로 어려워지고 있다.

editorial.body: 입력 업데이트 종합 26면 26일 붕괴 사고가 발생한 서울 서대문구 서소문 고가차도 철거 현장에서 소방 관계자들이 사고 수습 작업을 하고 있다. 연합뉴스 어제(26일) 오후 2시33분쯤 서울 서대문구 서소문 고가차도 철거 현장에서 상판 일부가 무너져 현장소장과 감리단장 등 공사 관계자 3명이 숨지고 3명이 다쳤다. 이 사고로 서울역~신촌역 구간을 비롯해 일부 열차 운행에도 차질이 빚어졌다. 안타까운 인명 피해가 발생한 만큼 현장을 조속히 수습하고, 사고 원인을 철저하게 규명해야 한다. 이번 사고는 어제 새벽 고가차도의 상부 바닥판(슬라브) 절단 작업 중 침하 현상을 발견하고 안전진단을 하는 과정에서 일어났다. 안전진단에는 숨진 현장소장과 감리단장, 서울시 관계자, 외부 전문가도 참여했다고 한다. 특히 이번 사고에선 위험 징후를 감지하고 철거를 중단한 뒤 관계자들이 모여 안전 상태를 확인하던 중 인명 피해가 발생했다는 점을 무겁게 받아들여야 한다. 안전진단 수칙을 지켰는지, 현장 통제 등 안전 조치는 충분했는지를 철저히 따져봐야 한다. 일부 사고 영상을 보면 고가 일부가 붕괴할 당시 아래 주변 도로로 차량이 지나가고 있었다. 자칫하면 더 큰 피해가 날 수도 있는 상황이었다. 서소문 고가차도는 1966년 개통된 노후 구조물로, 정밀 안전진단에서 D등급 판정을 받아 철거 후 재시공이 결정됐다. 애초에 구조적 취약성이 있는 시설인 만큼 철거 과정의 안전관리는 더 엄격했어야 했다. 발주 기관인 서울시와 시공사, 감리단이 각자의 책임을 다했는지도 종합적으로 살필 필요가 있다. 노후 시설의 철거는 일반 건설 공사보다 위험성이 크다. 해체 과정에서 구조물의 불안정성이 커지기 때문이다. 문제는 도심의 노후 시설 철거와 재시공이 늘어날 수밖에 없다는 점이다. 2021년 6월 광주광역시 동구 학동 재개발 구역에서는 철거 중이던 5층 건물이 도로 쪽으로 무너지면서 시내버스를 덮쳐 9명이 숨졌다. 오래된 시설을 방치할 수는 없지만 철거 과정에서 또 다른 위험을 만들어서도 안 된다. 이번 사고를 계기로 고위험 철거 공사의 안전 기준과 현장 감리 체계를 다시 점검해야 한다. 아울러 침하나 균열 등 위험 징후가 발견됐을 때 작업을 중단하는 것에 그치지 말고 현장 접근 제한과 교통 통제를 포함한 후속 조치를 신속히 시행하는 체계를 갖춰야 한다.
editorial.pullQuote: 애초에 구조적 취약성이 있는 시설인 만큼 철거 과정의 안전관리는 더 엄격했어야 했다.
summary: 서울 서소문 고가차도 철거 현장서 상판 붕괴, 공사 관계자 3명 사망.
안전진단 중 붕괴 발생, 구조적 취약성 있는 노후시설 관리 미흡.
고위험 철거공사 안전기준 재점검과 현장 감리체계 강화 필수.
editorial.stance: 노후시설 철거의 증가 추세 속에서 안전 기준 강화와 현장 감리 체계 전면 개선이 시급하다.
editorial.titleTr: [사설] 서소문 고가 붕괴 사망 사고, 또 드러난 안전 관리 허술
summaryTr: Deck collapses at Seoul Seosomun overpass demolition site, killing three construction workers.
Collapse occurs during safety inspection, revealing poor management of aging infrastructure with structural vulnerabilities.
Urgent need to reinspect safety standards for high-risk demolition work and strengthen on-site supervision systems.
editorial.bodyTr:
Yesterday afternoon at 2:33 p.m., a partial collapse occurred at the Seoseomun overpass demolition site in Seodaemun-gu, Seoul, resulting in the deaths of three construction workers, including the site manager and supervision director, with three others injured. The accident also disrupted train operations on several routes, including the Seoul Station to Sinchon Station line. Given the tragic loss of life, authorities must promptly address the situation and thoroughly investigate the cause of the accident.

The collapse occurred during a safety inspection process after settlement was detected while cutting the upper slab of the overpass early yesterday morning. The safety inspection involved the deceased site manager and supervision director, Seoul city officials, and external experts. Notably, the human casualties occurred while workers were gathered to verify safety conditions after detecting warning signs and halting demolition work. This raises serious questions about whether safety inspection protocols were properly followed and whether on-site safety measures, including traffic control, were adequate.

Some accident footage shows that vehicles were passing on nearby roads below when the overpass partially collapsed, a situation that could have resulted in far greater casualties. The Seoseomun overpass, which opened in 1966, is an aging structure that received a grade D rating in a detailed safety assessment, leading to a decision for demolition and reconstruction. Given the facility's inherent structural vulnerabilities, safety management during the demolition process should have been far more stringent. A comprehensive review is needed to assess whether Seoul city as the client, the contractor, and the supervision team all fulfilled their respective responsibilities.

Demolition of aging facilities carries greater risks than general construction projects because structural instability increases during the dismantling process. The concern is that demolition and reconstruction of aging urban facilities will inevitably increase. In June 2021, a five-story building being demolished in the Hakdong redevelopment zone in Dong-gu, Gwangju collapsed onto a city bus, killing nine people. While deteriorated facilities cannot be left unaddressed, we cannot create new dangers in the process of their removal.

This accident should serve as an opportunity to re-examine safety standards and on-site supervision systems for high-risk demolition work. Furthermore, when warning signs such as settlement or cracks are detected, authorities must establish a system that goes beyond simply halting work to promptly implement follow-up measures including restricting site access and implementing traffic control.

leanScore.label: Center
leanScore.rationale: 사설이 안전 관리 실패를 비판하면서도 특정 정치 진영이나 경제 체제를 겨냥하지 않고, 발주기관·시공사·감리단의 책임을 균형있게 지적하며 시스템 개선을 중립적으로 제안함.

editorial.title: Ballots cast in the dark
editorial.body: Education superintendent races dominated by populist pledges amid voter indifference In South Korea, elections for superintendents of education are held at the same time as local elections, but it is very common for voters to cast their ballots without even knowing what kind of people the superintendent candidates are. The upcoming June 3 election is no different. A total of 58 people are running as candidates for superintendent in 16 metropolitan and provincial offices of education. They took part in TV debates, but almost no one paid attention. In Seoul in particular, a record eight candidates entered the race. This number is not only the highest since the introduction of the direct election system for superintendents of education, but also the largest among all superintendent contests in this election. Voters are only left even more confused about which candidate to choose. Although political parties are prohibited from nominating candidates in the name of ensuring the independence of education from political power, the candidates are generally divided into conservative and progressive camps. The record number of candidates running for Seoul superintendent of education resulted from the fact that, although both camps attempted to unify their candidates, some refused to take part in their own primary process or rejected its outcome. Similar things happened in other regions. Defying agreements to unify candidates has become common in superintendent of education elections. There has also been a case in which someone ran again in a by-election for superintendent of education after being released through a special pardon. He was serving time for bribing a rival candidate — an election law violation that also cost him his superintendent post. In addition to moral condemnation of his shamelessness, he faced strong criticism in particular for running again while still having failed to repay most of the government campaign subsidies that must be returned under the election act if a violation is found. The populism of campaign promises — poured out indiscriminately by candidates across both progressive and conservative camps in an attempt to win voters’ favor with cash — is such that it puts that of politicians to shame. According to a survey by a daily newspaper, as many as 40 candidates made such pledges. Their promised cash support ranges from at least 100,000 won ($66) to as much as 50 million won per student. Many candidates promised to provide 1 million won per student upon entering middle or high school, to be invested in a fund that could then be used upon high school graduation to pay for college tuition or job preparation expenses. One candidate pledged fully free early childhood education, expenses for transportation to school and return to home, and full support for the costs of school trips and field learning activities. The promised support grew like an escalating wager, to the point that one candidate even proposed creating a "self-reliance fund" of up to 50 million won per student, by matching parents’ contributions with funds from the education office budget over the course of schooling from elementary to high school. There is even an absurd pledge to provide 200,000 won per student per month to cover after-school private education expenses, even though an education superintendent is responsible for the development of public education. One of the main reasons education superintendent candidates are able to make such pledges is a loophole in the local education financial grant system, under which 20.79 percent of internal tax revenue is automatically allocated to local education offices every year. As the number of students declines while the grant continues to increase each year, candidates end up making pledges that amount to cash handouts. Given the low level of voter interest, there is also likely an intention to win votes through populist, vote-buying-style pledges. The direct election system for superintendents of education, first implemented in 2007, no longer contributes to the development of education. Rather, its side effects are only growing amid voter indifference. summaryTr: 남한의 교육감 선거가 유권자 무관심과 사상 최다 후보 수로 몸살을 앓고 있다.
후보들이 학생 1명당 연 66달러에서 33,000달러에 이르는 과도한 현금 공약을 내걸고 있다.
교육 재정의 허점이 공교육의 질보다 표 확보를 우선하는 포퓰리즘적 공약을 가능하게 하고 있다.
editorial.bodyTr:
교육감 선거, 유권자 무관심 속 포퓰리즘 공약으로 점철

한국에서 교육감 선거는 지방선거와 동시에 치러지지만, 유권자들이 교육감 후보가 어떤 인물인지도 모른 채 투표하는 일이 매우 흔하다. 6월 3일로 예정된 이번 선거도 다르지 않다. 16개 시·도 교육청에서 총 58명이 교육감 후보로 출마했다. 이들은 텔레비전 토론회에 참가했지만 거의 아무도 관심을 두지 않았다. 특히 서울의 경우 사상 최다인 8명의 후보가 출마했다. 이 숫자는 교육감 직선제 도입 이후 가장 많을 뿐만 아니라 이번 선거의 모든 교육감 경선 중에서도 가장 많다. 유권자들은 어느 후보를 선택해야 할지 더욱 혼란스러워하고 있다. 정치권의 교육 독립성을 보장하기 위해 정당의 공식 후보 추천은 금지돼 있지만, 후보들은 일반적으로 보수와 진보 진영으로 나뉜다. 서울 교육감 선거에 사상 최다 인원이 출마한 것은 양 진영이 모두 후보 단일화를 시도했으나 일부가 자신의 경선 참여를 거부하거나 그 결과를 받아들이지 않았기 때문이다. 다른 지역에서도 비슷한 일이 발생했다. 교육감 선거에서 후보 단일화 합의를 무시하고 출마하는 것이 일상화됐다. 교육감 보궐선거에서 특별사면으로 출소한 뒤 다시 출마한 사람도 있었다. 그는 경쟁 후보에게 뇌물을 제공한 혐의로 복역 중이었는데, 이는 선거법 위반으로 교육감 직책을 잃게 만든 사건이었다. 그의 뻔뻔함에 대한 도덕적 규탄 외에도 선거법 위반 적발 시 반환해야 하는 정부 선거 보조금 대부분을 아직도 반환하지 않은 채 다시 출마한 것에 대해 강한 비판을 받았다. 캠페인 공약의 포퓰리즘은 진보와 보수 양 진영의 후보들이 현금으로 유권자의 호감을 얻으려는 시도 속에서 마구잡이로 쏟아져 나오고 있으며, 정치인들의 포퓰리즘도 부끄러워할 정도다. 한 일간지의 조사에 따르면 무려 40명의 후보가 그러한 공약을 제시했다. 약속된 현금 지원은 학생 1인당 최소 10만 원에서 최대 5천만 원에 달한다. 많은 후보들이 중고교 입학 시 학생 1인당 100만 원을 제공하고 이를 고등학교 졸업 후 대학 등록금이나 취업 준비 비용으로 사용할 수 있는 펀드에 투자할 것을 약속했다. 한 후보는 유아 교육 완전 무상 제공, 등하교 교통비, 학교 현장학습 및 체험학습 비용 전액 지원을 약속했다. 약속된 지원은 점점 과장되는 양상을 보여, 한 후보는 초등학교부터 고등학교까지 학부모 기여금과 교육청 예산을 매칭하는 방식으로 학생 1인당 최대 5천만 원의 '자립기금'을 만들 것을 제안하기도 했다. 교육감이 공교육 발전을 책임지는 역할을 하는 데도 불구하고, 학생 1인당 월 20만 원의 사교육비를 지원하겠다는 황당한 공약까지 나왔다. 교육감 후보들이 이러한 공약을 할 수 있는 주요 이유 중 하나는 지방교육재정교부금 제도의 허점이다. 이 제도에서는 내국세의 20.79%가 매년 자동으로 지방교육청에 배분된다. 학생 수가 감소하면서도 교부금이 매년 증가함에 따라 후보들은 현금을 나눠주는 수준의 공약을 제시하게 된다. 유권자의 관심도가 낮다는 점도 포퓰리즘적이고 표 매수 성격의 공약으로 표를 얻으려는 의도를 낳을 수 있다. 2007년 처음 도입된 교육감 직선제는 더 이상 교육 발전에 기여하지 못하고 있다. 오히려 유권자의 무관심 속에서 부작용만 커지고 있다.

editorials.title: Ballots cast in the dark
editorials.body: Education superintendent races dominated by populist pledges amid voter indifference In South Korea, elections for superintendents of education are held at the same time as local elections, but it is very common for voters to cast their ballots without even knowing what kind of people the superintendent candidates are. The upcoming June 3 election is no different. A total of 58 people are running as candidates for superintendent in 16 metropolitan and provincial offices of education. They took part in TV debates, but almost no one paid attention. In Seoul in particular, a record eight candidates entered the race. This number is not only the highest since the introduction of the direct election system for superintendents of education, but also the largest among all superintendent contests in this election. Voters are only left even more confused about which candidate to choose. Although political parties are prohibited from nominating candidates in the name of ensuring the independence of education from political power, the candidates are generally divided into conservative and progressive camps. The record number of candidates running for Seoul superintendent of education resulted from the fact that, although both camps attempted to unify their candidates, some refused to take part in their own primary process or rejected its outcome. Similar things happened in other regions. Defying agreements to unify candidates has become common in superintendent of education elections. There has also been a case in which someone ran again in a by-election for superintendent of education after being released through a special pardon. He was serving time for bribing a rival candidate — an election law violation that also cost him his superintendent post. In addition to moral condemnation of his shamelessness, he faced strong criticism in particular for running again while still having failed to repay most of the government campaign subsidies that must be returned under the election act if a violation is found. The populism of campaign promises — poured out indiscriminately by candidates across both progressive and conservative camps in an attempt to win voters’ favor with cash — is such that it puts that of politicians to shame. According to a survey by a daily newspaper, as many as 40 candidates made such pledges. Their promised cash support ranges from at least 100,000 won ($66) to as much as 50 million won per student. Many candidates promised to provide 1 million won per student upon entering middle or high school, to be invested in a fund that could then be used upon high school graduation to pay for college tuition or job preparation expenses. One candidate pledged fully free early childhood education, expenses for transportation to school and return to home, and full support for the costs of school trips and field learning activities. The promised support grew like an escalating wager, to the point that one candidate even proposed creating a "self-reliance fund" of up to 50 million won per student, by matching parents’ contributions with funds from the education office budget over the course of schooling from elementary to high school. There is even an absurd pledge to provide 200,000 won per student per month to cover after-school private education expenses, even though an education superintendent is responsible for the development of public education. One of the main reasons education superintendent candidates are able to make such pledges is a loophole in the local education financial grant system, under which 20.79 percent of internal tax revenue is automatically allocated to local education offices every year. As the number of students declines while the grant continues to increase each year, candidates end up making pledges that amount to cash handouts. Given the low level of voter interest, there is also likely an intention to win votes through populist, vote-buying-style pledges. The direct election system for superintendents of education, first implemented in 2007, no longer contributes to the development of education. Rather, its side effects are only growing amid voter indifference. summaryTr: 남한의 교육감 선거가 유권자 무관심과 사상 최다 후보 수로 몸살을 앓고 있다.
후보들이 학생 1명당 연 66달러에서 33,000달러에 이르는 과도한 현금 공약을 내걸고 있다.
교육 재정의 허점이 공교육의 질보다 표 확보를 우선하는 포퓰리즘적 공약을 가능하게 하고 있다.

editorials.title: Beyond hype
editorials.body: Popular state-backed fund draws cash but raises concerns over long-term discipline The line formed before the doors opened. By the end of Friday, most of the National Growth Fund’s retail tranche had already been taken up. Nearly 87 percent of the 600 billion won ($398.9 million) quota was gone in a day, and the tech-heavy Kosdaq market shot up 4.99 percent in response. The spectacle was striking. But its implications are less obvious, at least for now. The fund arrives at a moment when the Lee Jae Myung administration’s policy ambition and strong market momentum overlap. Competition in semiconductors and artificial intelligence is intensifying, while domestic equities hover near their highs. Against this backdrop, policymakers are attempting to redirect private savings into strategic industries, hoping to narrow asset gaps while financing long-horizon technologies. In scale, the effort is formidable. The plan is to mobilize up to 150 trillion won over five years, combining public and private capital across sectors from AI to biotech. The logic is that, left alone, private finance is unlikely to supply sufficient patient capital to industries with long payback periods and uncertain returns. The appeal to investors is equally clear. The government said it will absorb up to 20 percent of losses, reshaping downside risk. Tax incentives reinforce the attraction, including income deductions of up to 40 percent and reduced taxation on dividends. For households shut out of large-cap rallies, the fund offers access to earlier-stage growth. Demand from lower- and middle-income investors has been particularly strong, reaching roughly 40 percent of bank subscriptions, well above the initial allocation. Yet popularity is not proof of durability. Even the launch revealed strain. Subscription channels opened at different times, and technical bottlenecks limited access. These are operational flaws, but they hint at a deeper issue: A policy instrument encountering demand at a scale it is not yet equipped to manage. The more consequential response has been official enthusiasm. Authorities are already considering additional issuance, potentially advancing future quotas into the current year. The temptation is understandable. It is also risky. Accelerating supply would complicate fiscal planning, given the public funds needed to support loss coverage and tax benefits. More importantly, it would push capital into a market already flush with liquidity. Korea’s constraint is not funding but investable assets. As multiple policy-driven funds converge on a narrow pool of unlisted technology firms and specialized Kosdaq companies, competition has intensified. Valuations have risen sharply, in some cases doubling or tripling within a year. In extreme cases, firms now dictate terms to investors. That inversion can carry dire consequences. Reports have emerged of weaker firms seeking short-term financing on the assumption that future policy allocations will provide repayment. When capital begins to anticipate policy rather than performance, discipline weakens. This pattern is not new. Earlier state-backed funds struggled to generate returns once fiscal support was stripped away or political priorities shifted. Their weakness lay less in intent than in execution. Capital was raised first, while investment discipline followed, if at all. Avoiding a sorry repeat of those failures will require restraint and balance. The test of success is not how quickly money is raised, but how well it is deployed. Fund managers must be allowed to invest at their own pace, guided by opportunity rather than administrative targets. Lower utilization, when quality assets are scarce, should be accepted rather than penalized. Flexibility will also matter. Annual funding should adjust to market conditions, and the investment limit should broaden where appropriate. Expanding into adjacent sectors could ease pressure on a narrow set of assets without diluting strategic focus. The state-led fund has tapped into a real demand: broader access to growth and a shift toward capital-led wealth formation. But the opening-day rush should not become the benchmark. If anything, a warning endures: In state finance, speed is easy to manufacture, discipline is not. By the end of Friday, most of the National Growth Fund’s retail tranche had already been taken up. Nearly 87 percent of the 600 billion won ($398.9 million) quota was gone in a day, and the tech-heavy Kosdaq market shot up 4.99 percent in response. The spectacle was striking. But its implications are less obvious, at least for now. The fund arrives at a moment when the Lee Jae Myung administration’s policy ambition and strong market momentum overlap. Competition in semiconductors and artificial intelligence is intensifying, while domestic equities hover near their highs. Against this backdrop, policymakers are attempting to redirect private savings into strategic industries, hoping to narrow asset gaps while financing long-horizon technologies. In scale, the effort is formidable. The plan is to mobilize up to 150 trillion won over five years, combining public and private capital across sectors from AI to biotech. The logic is that, left alone, private finance is unlikely to supply sufficient patient capital to industries with long payback periods and uncertain returns. The appeal to investors is equally clear. The government said it will absorb up to 20 percent of losses, reshaping downside risk. Tax incentives reinforce the attraction, including income deductions of up to 40 percent and reduced taxation on dividends. For households shut out of large-cap rallies, the fund offers access to earlier-stage growth. Demand from lower- and middle-income investors has been particularly strong, reaching roughly 40 percent of bank subscriptions, well above the initial allocation. Yet popularity is not proof of durability. Even the launch revealed strain. Subscription channels opened at different times, and technical bottlenecks limited access. These are operational flaws, but they hint at a deeper issue: A policy instrument encountering demand at a scale it is not yet equipped to manage. The more consequential response has been official enthusiasm. Authorities are already considering additional issuance, potentially advancing future quotas into the current year. The temptation is understandable. It is also risky. Accelerating supply would complicate fiscal planning, given the public funds needed to support loss coverage and tax benefits. More importantly, it would push capital into a market already flush with liquidity. Korea’s constraint is not funding but investable assets. As multiple policy-driven funds converge on a narrow pool of unlisted technology firms and specialized Kosdaq companies, competition has intensified. Valuations have risen sharply, in some cases doubling or tripling within a year. In extreme cases, firms now dictate terms to investors. That inversion can carry dire consequences. Reports have emerged of weaker firms seeking short-term financing on the assumption that future policy allocations will provide repayment. When capital begins to anticipate policy rather than performance, discipline weakens. This pattern is not new. Earlier state-backed funds struggled to generate returns once fiscal support was stripped away or political priorities shifted. Their weakness lay less in intent than in execution. Capital was raised first, while investment discipline followed, if at all. Avoiding a sorry repeat of those failures will require restraint and balance. The test of success is not how quickly money is raised, but how well it is deployed. Fund managers must be allowed to invest at their own pace, guided by opportunity rather than administrative targets. Lower utilization, when quality assets are scarce, should be accepted rather than penalized. Flexibility will also matter. Annual funding should adjust to market conditions, and the investment limit should broaden where appropriate. Expanding into adjacent sectors could ease pressure on a narrow set of assets without diluting strategic focus. The state-led fund has tapped into a real demand: broader access to growth and a shift toward capital-led wealth formation. But the opening-day rush should not become the benchmark. If anything, a warning endures: In state finance, speed is easy to manufacture, discipline is not. summaryTr: Government concludes Namu Ho was struck by Iranian anti-ship missile
Iran's denial expected to prolong resolution of the issue
Strong apology and responsible measures demanded
editorials.bodyTr: The government has concluded that the aircraft that struck the Korean vessel HMM Namu in the Strait of Hormuz was likely an Iranian anti-ship missile. However, with Iran flatly denying its involvement, resolving the matter is expected to take considerable time. Strong demands must be made of Iran for an apology and responsible follow-up measures.

editorials.titleTr: [Op-Ed] "Korea Is a 'Dagger' to China," Says U.S. Commander in Korea, Crossing the Line Again
summaryTr: The commander of U.S. Forces Korea described South Korea as a 'dagger' to counter China.
The remarks by a commander with operational control authority are expected to spark controversy.
Politically provocative statements by high-ranking U.S. military officials could escalate into a diplomatic issue.
editorials.bodyTr: Commander of U.S. Forces in Korea Jay B. Bartlett has expressed his perception that Korea would appear to China as a "dagger" aimed at them. The Commander of U.S. Forces in Korea, who would exercise operational control of South Korea-U.S. combined forces in the event of war on the Korean Peninsula, has characterized the significance of military capabilities deployed in Korea as posing a threat to China.

editorial.titleTr: Huawei's Semiconductor Manufacturing Without EUV Challenges Samsung's Performance-Driven Strategy
summaryTr: Samsung clinches wage deal focused on performance bonuses...up to 600 million won per employee
Huawei succeeds in developing advanced chip manufacturing technology without EUV equipment
Korean and Chinese semiconductor firms choose entirely different paths
editorial.bodyTr: Samsung Electronics and Huawei, semiconductor companies representing South Korea and China respectively, made announcements of entirely different character just two days apart. On the 27th, Samsung Electronics' labor and management signed off on a wage agreement in which the union effectively controls the criteria for performance bonuses, with employees receiving up to 600 million won per person in performance pay. Meanwhile, two days earlier, Huawei announced technology capable of manufacturing advanced chips without "extreme ultraviolet (EUV) lithography equipment," which had been considered essential for cutting-edge semiconductor processes.

leanScore.rationale: 노조의 성과급 기준 결정권을 비판적으로 표현('좌지우지')하며 삼성의 임금협상을 부정적으로 프레이밍하고, 이에 대비되는 화웨이의 기술 성과를 긍정적으로 묘사하는 방식은 노조 강경을 문제시하는 보수적 관점을 드러냄.

editorials.titleTr: Seoul residents forced out as soaring housing costs spark 'involuntary exodus
summaryTr: Last month, Seoul's net population outflow reached 6,341 people, marking the largest figure in 18 months.
Rising housing prices are accelerating involuntary departures by residents.
Population exodus driven by soaring housing prices is not a normal phenomenon.
editorials.bodyTr: 지난달 서울에서 순유출된 인구가 6341명으로 18개월 만에 최대로 늘어난 것으로 나타났다. 서울의 인구 순유출은 이미 오래됐지만, 집값 상승과 맞물려 증가폭이 다시 커진 것이다. 물론 양질의 일자리를 찾아 이주하는 경우는 바람직하다고 볼 수도 있다. 하지만 집값 상승으로 서울에서 밀려나는 시민이 늘어나는 현상을 정상이라고 할 수는 없을 것이다. 27일 국..

editorials.title: 21개월 연속 '아기 울음' 증가, 인구 반등 골든타임 잘 살려야
editorials.body: 지난 3월 전국 출생아 수가 크게 늘었다. 혼인도 꾸준히 증가해 8년 만에 가장 높은 수준으로 올라섰다는 소식이다. 어렵사리 찾아온 혼인·출산의 상승 흐름이 구조적 추세로 자리 잡을 수 있도록 세심하고 과감한 정책 지원이 그 어느 때보다 긴요해졌다. 국가데이터처 집계를 보면 3월 출생아 수는 총 2만5200명으로 전년 동월 대비 19.4% 늘었다. 21개월..
editorials.pullQuote: 어렵사리 찾아온 혼인·출산의 상승 흐름이 구조적 추세로 자리 잡을 수 있도록 세심하고 과감한 정책 지원이 필요하다.
summary: 3월 출생아 2만5200명, 전년 동월 대비 19.4% 증가했다.
혼인도 8년 만에 최고 수준으로 올라 긍정 신호를 보이고 있다.
인구 반등의 골든타임을 살리려면 정책 지원이 절실하다.
editorials.stance: 인구 감소 위기 극복을 위해 현재의 긍정적 흐름을 정책적으로 뒷받침해야 한다는 입장
editorials.titleTr: Consecutive 21-Month Rise in Births: South Korea Must Seize Golden Window for Population Rebound
summaryTr: Babies born in March numbered 25,200, a 19.4% increase compared to the same month last year.
Marriages also rose to their highest level in eight years, showing positive signals.
Policy support is urgently needed to capitalize on this golden opportunity for population rebound.
editorials.bodyTr: The number of births nationwide surged significantly in March. Marriages have also been steadily increasing, reaching their highest level in eight years. As this long-awaited upward trend in marriages and births has finally emerged, careful and bold policy support is more crucial than ever to establish it as a structural trend. According to data compiled by the National Statistics Office, the number of births in March totaled 25,200, up 19.4 percent compared to the same month last year. For the first time in 21 months...

leanScore.rationale: 출산 증가를 긍정적으로 보되 정책 지원의 필요성만 강조할 뿐 특정 이념적 입장(복지 확대/규제 강화 vs 시장중심)을 명시하지 않으며, 좌우 모두에게 수용 가능한 인구정책 프레임을 유지함.

editorial.title: [사설] 합계출산율 3개월째 0.9명대…증가세 유지가 관건
summary: 합계출산율이 3개월 연속 0.9명대를 유지하고 있다.
저출산 위기 속에서 증가세를 지속하는 것이 핵심 과제다.
정책 효과를 높이기 위한 실질적 대안이 필요하다.
editorial.stance: 저출산 위기 극복을 위해 증가세를 지속시키고 실효성 있는 정책 강화가 시급하다.
editorial.titleTr: Opinion: Fertility Rate Holds at 0.9 for Third Month...Sustaining Growth Is Key
summaryTr: South Korea's total fertility rate has remained below 1.0 for three consecutive months, hovering in the 0.9 range.
Sustaining growth amid the low birth rate crisis is a key challenge.
Concrete alternatives are needed to enhance the effectiveness of government policies.
